folio.label: 154v
folio.l: That ©e in hele , ar comen hom a geyn
And if that I were riche , as haue I blisse
Of twenty thousand sheldes , shuld ©e nat mysse
For ©e so kyndely , this other day
Lent me gold , and as I can and may
I thanke ©ow by god , and by Seynt Iame
But nathelees , I toke vn to oure dame
ñoure wyf at hom , the same gold a geyn
Vp on ©oure benche , she wot it wel certeyn
By certeyn tokenes , that I can ©ow telle
Now by ©oure leeue , I may no lengere dwelle
Oure Abbot wyl out , of this toun a noon
And in his compaignye , mot I gon
Grete wel oure dame , my~ owne nece swete
And fare wel dere Cosyn , til we mete
This Marchau~t , whicú that was , ful war & wys
Creaunced hatú , and paied eke in Parys
To certeyn lumbardes , redy in here hond
The so~me of gold , and gat of hem his bond
And home he gotú mirie , as a Popingay
For wel he knew , he stod in swicú array
That nedes must he wy~ne , in that viage
A thousand frankes , a boue al his costage
His wyf ful redy mette hi~ , atte gate
As she was wont , of olde vsage algate
And al that nygút , in myrthe they ben sette
For he was riche , and clerely out of dette
¢ Whan it was day , this Marchau~t gan embrace
His wyf al newe , and kist hire on hire face
And vp he gotú , and maketú it ful tougú
Nomore quod she , by god ©e han I now
And wantounly a geyn , witú hi~ she pleide
Til atte laste , this Marchaunt seide
By god quod he , I am a litel wrotú
Witú ©ow my wyf , al thougú it me be lotú
And wot ©e why , by god as that I gesse
That ©e han made , a manäe straungenesse
Bitwixen me , and my Cosyn , Daun Ioún
ñe shulde han warned me , or I had gon
That he ©ow hadde , an hondred frankes paid
By redy tokene , and held hi~ euele aapaid
For that I to hi~ spak , of cheuysaunce
Me semed so , as by his countenaunce
But nathelees , by god , oure heuene kyngö
